page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7se.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at a base frequency of 2.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance consistently in the average performance tier. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1871.18 and a multi-core score of 11658.63. Memory performance is supported by a 48 MB L3 cache, yielding a read-only small-block memory bandwidth of 639.69 GB/sec and a large-block write bandwidth of 53.59 GB/sec. In database operations, the instance processes 11.97 million Redis SET operations per second and achieves a PassMark database score of 6125.12. For language processing, it handles small-model (135M) LLM prompt processing at 1065.14 tokens per second, though performance drops to a weaker 24.38 tokens per second for medium (7B) models.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density, the ecs.r7se.3xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage or GPU accelerators. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale network storage independently. This configuration is highly suitable for in-memory databases like Redis, enterprise applications, caching layers, and moderate data processing pipelines that require a high RAM-to-CPU ratio but do not demand high-speed local NVMe drives.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7se.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated x86_64 cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio, while omitting local storage and GPU hardware. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results place its performance in the average tier, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 11658.63 and a PassMark database score of 6125.12. It processes Redis SET operations at 11.97 million operations per second. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications that benefit from dedicated compute resources and high memory density without requiring local storage.

tagline: A dedicated memory-optimized instance featuring a high RAM-to-CPU ratio for demanding in-memory databases and enterprise caching workloads.

96.0 GB of system memory providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and enterprise applications
